title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
pattern: Dynamic Programming â€” DP with Binary Search Optimization
coreIdea: Find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in an array. The naive approach compares every pair; the optimized approach maintains a list of smallest tail values for increasing subsequences of each length, using binary search to place new elements.
recognitionCues: strictly or non-strictly increasing condition specified
approach: Naive O(n^2): dp[i] = length of LIS ending at i, computed by checking all j < i where arr[j] < arr[i]. Optimized O(n log n): maintain a 'tails' array, for each element use binary search to find its position (replace or extend), final length of tails array is the answer.
timeComplexity: O(n^2) naive, O(n log n) optimized
